english: n. of one of the thirty two characteristics of Buddha;
"possessed of seven": a) sun (as possessed the seven horses in it's chariot); b) perfume (consisting of seven ingredients); c) offering.
russian: "обладающий семью": а) солнце (как обладающее семью лошадьми в своей колеснице); б) благовоние (состоящее из семи ингредиентов); в) жертвоприношение

english: the seventh.
religious robe of the Buddhist monk.
of seven parts; seven in number.
week, seven days.
to compare.
happiness, felicity.
1) "Born by happiness" (epitn. of Maheśvara); 2) n. of a bird.
poet. skull; drinking cup made of the human skull.
russian: поэт. череп; чаша из черепа (букв. защитник блага)

english: buddh. going to happiness; state of happiness;
russian: будд. движение к благу; состояние блага

english: "having state of happiness" (epith. of gods);
"three states of happiness" (born in the world of human beings, gods and asuras);
russian: "три благих состояния" (рождение в мире людей, богов, асуров)

english: "place of happiness": a) heaven; b) god;
russian: "благостное место": а) небеса, рай; б) божество

english: poet. good deed (lit. path to happiness).
russian: поэт. благое деяние (букв. путь к счастью)

english: 1) buddh. blessed; happy; 2) n. of the paradise of Amitābha;
russian: 1) будд. благословенный; счастливый; 2) назв. рая Амитабхи

english: n. of the realm of Śambhala.
1) one who feels healthy or happy; 2) n. of a sect.
1) great happiness; 2) buddh. n. of one of the three elements of the Vajrayani-nirvāṇa (the others are śūnya and vijñāna); 3) buddh. eternal happiness or the creative principle of the Cosmos;
buddh. a form of Vajrayāna;
"Island of happiness" (n. of a monastery in Amdo);
"the King of happiness" (epith. of Padmasambhava, the precursor of the Nying-ma sect in Tibet).
russian: "Царь счастья" (эпит. Падмасамбхавы, предтечи школы Ньингма в Тибете).

english: "One in great happiness" (epith. of Buddha).
